checks.finding: REQ-001 through REQ-011 define measurable routing, isolation, manifest, reporting, runtime-equivalence, and release outcomes; AC-001 through AC-007 and TEST-001 through TEST-006 provide corresponding observable validation surfaces.

The ordered cost-aware routing goal is covered by AC-001 and TEST-001, while bounded file-backed implementation and independent-review contexts are covered by AC-002 through AC-004 and TEST-002 through TEST-003.

AC-001 through AC-007 specify inspectable structural-test results, identifiers, diagnostics, manifest acceptance and rejection, template fields, release values, repository validation, and rollback behavior.

The Non-goals explicitly exclude universally pinned model identifiers, reviewer or evaluator fallback, and chat-log task inputs; REQ-001, REQ-005, and REQ-006 further bound role routing and host-specific fallback.

Material capability, availability, approval, fail-closed, deterministic-runtime, session-isolation, immutable-snapshot, symlink, cost-provenance, compatibility, and version constraints are explicitly stated in REQ-001 through REQ-011.

High-risk escalation, terminal blocking and reapproval, unavailable-tier and runtime failures, context reuse, manifest tampering, cross-runtime equivalence, and rollback integrity have explicit validation surfaces in AC-001 through AC-007 and TEST-001 through TEST-006.
